title: CV & Experience
text:
Research & Professional Appointments


Data Scientist & Research Software Engineer Jan 2026 – Present


Jean Golding Institute, University of Bristol

Providing data science and research software engineering support on collaborative university projects:

Package Development: Developed and maintain the digiqual Python package for Non-Destructive Evaluation reliability assessment in EEME.
Statistical Analysis: Provide statistics and model-building support to the Diabetes and Metabolism team at the Bristol Medical School.
Data Science Support: Work on data pipelines and analysis for the PPADEM Project in the School of Sociology, Politics and International Studies.




Research Associate in Probabilistic Programming Sep 2024 – Dec 2025


The Alan Turing Institute, London

Research on applied statistical methods in ecology and evolution for the SPHERE-PPL project:

Community Software: Developed and managed open-source forecasting contests, back-end scoring tools, and developer starter templates.
Statistical Modelling: Built Bayesian Hierarchical models and refined Phylogenetic Generalised Linear Mixed Models (PGLMMs) to evaluate biodiversity hotspots and trait-environment filtering.
Event Organization: Co-organized the Health Modelling & Forecasting Workshop (Jan 2025) at the British Library.




Senior Consultant, Strategic Analytics Mar 2023 – Sep 2024


AtkinsRèalis, Bristol

Delivered data-driven decision-making and operational research solutions: - Software Capability Lead: Managed the development of custom analytical tools in R, line-managing several teams of analysts. - Operational Research: Served as Simulation Workstream Lead for Heathrow Security and Manchester Airport Group security programs.



Simulation Modelling Specialist & Principal Analyst 2019 – 2021


BNSSG CCG, National Health Service (NHS), Bristol

Developed analytical packages and user interfaces in R to model pathway capacity and patient flow:

PathSimR Package: Developed the PathSimR discrete event simulation (DES) package and Shiny application.
Operational Analysis: Modeled patient pathways, telehealth implementations, and COVID-19 vaccination clinic queues using queueing theory and stochastic modelling.




Research Assistant Aug 2016 – Dec 2018


Museum für Naturkunde, Berlin, Germany

Collaborated on DFG-funded research tracing niche evolution across African Savannah herbivores (Bovidae):

Fieldwork: Led morphometric data collection campaigns in Nairobi, Kenya and Addis Ababa, Ethiopia.




Education


PhD in Biology 2019 – 2023


Milner Centre for Evolution, University of Bath, UK


Thesis: “Phylogenetic Diversity, Morphological Disparity & Niche Adaptation in Seabirds”
Supervisors: Prof. Matt Wills, Dr. Nick Priest (Bath) & Dr. Jane Younger (Tasmania).
Key Methods: R programming, geometric morphometrics, phylogenetic comparative methods, and species delimitation.




Master of Earth Sciences (MEarthSci) 2011 – 2015


Exeter College, University of Oxford, UK


Classification: 2.i (First Class Masters Thesis on Pterosaur Macroevolution).
Fieldwork: Pembrokeshire, Arran, Dorset, Assynt, Corsica, Spain, and Bermuda.




Training & Teaching Materials


PGLMMs for Biodiversity Modelling Workshop StanCon 2024


Workshop Developer & Instructor

Created and delivered training materials on using Stan and R to build Phylogenetic Generalised Linear Mixed Models (PGLMMs) for species trait distribution modelling.



Maths for Life Sciences Imperial College London (2025)


Course Instructor

Taught foundational mathematical techniques, differential equations, and statistical modelling to first-year undergraduate Life Sciences students.



SPHERE-PPL Forecasting Contest Materials Alan Turing Institute (24-25)


Lead Developer

Developed notebooks, tutorials, and starter code for researchers entering probabilistic forecasting challenges.



Grants & Awards

Research Grant (£20,000 Consumable Grant) | Royal Society London (2021)
Visiting Research Scholarship ($3,900 Travel Grant) | Chicago Field Museum (2020)
Future Research Leaders Grant (£2,509 Travel Grant) | University of Bath (2019)
Evolution Education Trust PhD Studentship | University of Bath (2019)



Student Supervision

BSc Biotechnology Co-Supervisor | Imperial College London (24/25)

Project: Understanding and forecasting NHS A&E patient load (Student: Gabriela Lemos Vargas)

BA Biology Co-Supervisor | University of Bath (21/22)

Project: Morphological Variation in Skeletal Elements (Students: Jack Hardy, Sam Jones, Harley Mitchell)
Project: Phylogeography & Cryptic Species in Malagasy Birds (Students: Georgina Russell, Louis Martin, Nefeli Karonis)

MSc Bioinformatics Co-Supervisor | University of Bath (21/22)

Project: Global Patterns of Biodiversity and Diversification in Fungi (Students: Gabriel Crabb, Enea Jorgji)

MSci Palaeobiology Thesis Co-Supervisor | University of Bristol (20/21)

Project: Locomotor evolution in Miocene horses (Student: Gwen Franklin)




Professional Development & Workshops

Statistical Rethinking: Course on Bayesian Data Analysis & Causal Inference (Online, 2023)
SVPCA 3D Data Workshop: Morphosource & FAIR principles (University of Oxford, 2021)
Public Engagement with Science Workshop: (The Royal Society, UK, 2020)
Phylogenetics in Evolution and Ecology Course: (Natural History Museum, Berlin, 2018)

title: Projects
text: A selection of research software packages, data science tools, and quantitative projects I have developed or contributed to.

title: Projects
text:
digiqual
A Python library developed and maintained for the UNDT group in the School of Electrical, Electronic and Mechanical Engineering at the University of Bristol. It provides a framework for Non-Destructive Evaluation (NDE) reliability assessment, implementing the Generalised \(\hat{a}\)-versus-\(a\) method to calculate probability of detection (PoD) curves.
Key features:

Active learning workflows using bootstrap committees to identify regions of high model uncertainty.
Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) for experimental design optimization.
Support for multi-dimensional marginalization of stochastic parameters (e.g., surface roughness).


Documentation GitHub Repository

title: Projects
text:
PPADEM
Data Science and Management support provided to the Political Parties in Africa DEMocracy (PPADEM) project at the School of Sociology, Politics and International Studies, University of Bristol, developing reproducible statistical workflows around digital political communications.

Project Website

title: Projects
text:
PathSimR
An R package and Shiny UI developed for modelling patient flow and pathway capacity in NHS organisations. It uses discrete event simulation (DES) and queueing theory to run “what-if” analyses, allowing managers to estimate waiting times and identify service bottlenecks.

GitHub Repository Journal Article

title: Projects
text:
SPHERE-PPL Contests
Open-source forecasting contests, scoring tools, and training materials developed for the SPHERE-PPL project at the Alan Turing Institute. The work supported Bayesian Hierarchical and Phylogenetic Generalised Linear Mixed Models (PGLMMs) to predict biodiversity hotspots.

Project Website

title: Projects
text:
Evolution of Diving in Waterbirds
Macroevolutionary analysis investigating the repeated convergence of diving within Waterbirds. Uses phylogenetic comparative methods in R to model rates and directionality of evolution.

Proc B Publication

title: Projects
text:
Albatross Bill Shape
Quantitative morphometric analysis investigating the shape variation in the compound bills of albatrosses. Uses phylogenetic comparative methods in R to study bill shape adaptation relative to diet and feeding strategy.

RSOS Publication

title: Projects
text:
Gentoo Penguin Species
Integrative species delimitation using genetic and morphometric datasets in R. This research demonstrated that the Gentoo Penguin is comprised of four distinct, morphologically and genetically isolated species.

Ecol Evol Publication Conversation Article

title: Dr Josh Tyler
text:
Data Scientist & Research Software Engineer

Jean Golding Institute, University of Bristol
I am a Data Scientist and Research Software Engineer (RSE) at the Jean Golding Institute, University of Bristol. My work focuses on building open-source research software, statistical modelling, and providing data science support across academic disciplines.
I have a background in evolutionary biology, operational research, and healthcare simulation, with a focus on developing packages and analytical pipelines in Python and R.
